mnemonics.title: Stack Operations Memory
mnemonics.content: PUSH adds at TOP, POP takes from TOP. Remember: Push goes IN, Pop comes OUT.

mnemonics.title: Queue Operations Memory
mnemonics.content: ENQUEUE at REAR, DEQUEUE from FRONT. Remember: Enter at End, Delete from Front.

mnemonics.title: Circular Queue Formula
mnemonics.content: New Position = (Current Position + 1) % MAXSIZE - Use MOD to wrap around!

acronyms.usage: Describes stack behavior - last element added is first to be removed
Describes queue behavior - first element added is first to be removed
In queue, front pointer indicates where elements are removed
In queue, rear pointer indicates where elements are added
Used to view element at stack top without modifying the stack

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a stack of plates - you add (push) and remove (pop) only from the TOP. Think of a vertical tube with a spring at bottom.
Think of a line at ticket counter - people join (enqueue) at end of line and leave (dequeue) from the front.
Imagine a circular track where the end connects back to the beginning. When rear reaches end, it wraps to start.
Write expression and underline operators in order of evaluation - reverse this order for postfix

analogies.analogy: Like a stack of books on a desk - you can only add or remove the top book.
Like people waiting in bank queue - first person in line gets service first.
Like seats in a round conference table - when one end is full, you wrap around to empty seats at the other end.
Like a full parking lot - no more cars can enter until some leave.
